purpose: Bootstrap module for the python group; installs system Python support packages via apt, then pipx, uv, ruff, ipython, and jupyterlab via pipx — establishing the three-tier Python ownership model (apt for system Python, pipx for global CLIs, uv for project deps).
invariants: System Python is only augmented with venv + dev support via apt — never pip-installed into
All Python CLIs (uv, ruff, ipython, jupyterlab) are installed via pipx into isolated venvs
uv is itself installed via pipx (not as a system package), so it can be upgraded with 'pipx upgrade uv'
No pytest, mypy, or project libraries are installed globally — those are always project-local
gotchas: Deliberately omits pyenv — uv manages Python version downloads itself (uv python install); running pyenv alongside would duplicate version management
jupyterlab is installed globally as a launch point only — Jupyter kernels stay project-local (each project runs 'uv add --dev jupyterlab' and registers its own kernel)
ruff replaces both black and flake8 — do not install either of those globally
pipx is installed via apt (not pip) so it bootstraps cleanly without needing pip or an existing pipx

key_functions.name: python_install
key_functions.summary: Installs system Python support (apt), then pipx, uv, ruff, ipython, and jupyterlab as isolated pipx-managed CLIs

key_functions.name: python_record_manifest
key_functions.summary: Upserts manifest entries for python3, pipx, uv, ruff, ipython, and jupyterlab with their install methods and detect commands
